Therefore a burn ban has been placed in effect for the next 14 days.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong southerly winds developed ahead of a cold front and caused low level shear to increase across the area.  As the front moved east the shear diminished.  By the time the storms moved into the Florida Panhandle, the event had transitioned from a tornado to a wind event.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: High winds from a thunderstorm blew down several trees and power lines near Milton.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong southerly winds developed ahead of a cold front and caused low level shear to increase across the area.  As the front moved east the shear diminished.  By the time the storms moved into the Florida Panhandle, the event had transitioned from a tornado to a wind event.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: High winds from a thunderstorm blew down several trees and power lines near Crestview.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong thunderstorm spawned a weak tornado in the Bayou George community.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A weak tornado briefly touched down off County Road 2301 in Bayou George. The majority of the damage was confined to uprooted trees.  There was structural damage to a few sheds, signs and a fence on Wood Place, Kent and Kisser Roads. A bathtub was picked up and hurled 200 feet. Many homes lost power as trees fell onto utility poles.  The damage survey was conducted by the NWS.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of severe thunderstorms ahead of a cold front moved through the Florida Big Bend Friday evening, October 27th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Some trees and power lines down northwest of Perry.  Reported by the Florida DOT.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An ongoing dry period has continued from early January through the current date, 15 December 2006. Many ASOS and CO-OP stations are 8 to 14 inches below normal for the year to date.  Aerial precipitation analysis from the Southeast River Forecast Center indicates large portions of the north Florida area are over 20 inches below normal for the year to date (12/15/06). This is especially true over most of the area between the Suwannee and St Johns Rivers. Significant portions of Baker, Bradford and Union Counties have only received 25 to 50 percent of normal annual precipitation.  Additionally, most areas within 10 miles of the St Johns River exhibit a similar rainfall deficit.  |In Nassau County, Florida the Ag Agent reports, extended drought with the St Mary���s River very low, and canoe rentals above St. George, Georgia not possible. Hay production this fall is significantly reduced,and winter forage will be a challenge unless we get some significant rain. They are anticipating paying higher prices for hay and losses if selling of cattle becomes necessary.|In Duval County, Florida, the impact of the drought is related to Air Pollution Response and the dust problem. The lack of rain combined with cleared construction sites has caused an increase in fugitive emissions.  This has impacted homeowners with larger cleaning bills with reference to electronic air cleaners and home upkeep. The construction industry has also experienced delays in land clearing and disposal of the debris due to non-burn days in the County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: In Nassau County, Florida the Ag Agent reports, extended drought with the St Mary���s River very low, and canoe rentals above St. George, Georgia not possible. Hay production this fall is significantly reduced,and winter forage will be a challenge unless we get some significant rain. They are anticipating paying higher prices for hay and losses if selling of cattle becomes necessary.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A squall line moved out of the Florida Big Bend and Panhandle with some severe weather in the Suwannee Valley. Damage was generally confined to trees and power lines down.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several trees down in the Suwannee River State Park with some down in the campground area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A squall line moved out of the Florida Big Bend and Panhandle with some severe weather in the Suwannee Valley. Damage was generally confined to trees and power lines down.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several trees down in the Trenton, Fl. area. Tree down across road at at Southwest 85th Lane. A second tree down across the road at Southwest 40th Street.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm wind gusts caused minor roof and siding damage to several homes in the Riverside Drive and Cleveland areas.  The winds also downed several tree limbs that downed power lines.
Same as Alabama.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong southerly winds brought coastal flooding to low lying areas along the coast.  Above normal tides and beach erosion were observed from Fort Pickens to Destin.  The tides were high enough to flood several of the roads along Santa Rosa Island, especially near Fort Pickens and Navarre Beach.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A squall line moved out of the Florida Big Bend and Panhandle with some severe weather in the Suwannee Valley. Damage was generally confined to trees and power lines down.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trained spotter, with rain gage, reported 2.25 inches of rain with squal line passage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong high pressure built to the north of the region with a tight presure gradient over the coastal waters from North Carolina to Florida. This developed strong north to northeasterly winds and a coastal trough along the Florida east coast.  These conditions are known locally as a northeaster. High surf and minor beach erosion occurred in Duval and Nassau Counties of northeast Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: From Talbot Island State Park, on Fort George Island they experienced flooding of roadways at high tide with some beach erosion.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of severe thunderstorms ahead of a cold front moved through the Florida Big Bend Friday evening, October 27th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Some trees and power lines down just west and northwest of Mayo.  Reported by the Florida DOT.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Warm moist, unstable air in advance of approaching cold front produced several tornadoes across the eastern Florida Panhandle and Big Bend on Friday afternoon and evening, October 27th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A waterspout came ashore near U.S. Highway 98 and 26th Street.  The tornado then tracked northeast through Apalachicola, and lifted near Water Street and Avenue G.  A few boats at a marina were capsized.  Four homes were destroyed and four dozen others were damaged.  Hundreds of trees were either knocked down or had their tops sheared off, and numerous utility poles were down.  The main electrical power substation was damaged, causing a citywide power outage.  All telephone and TV cable lines were down.  A restaurant along U.S. Highway 98 sustained heavy damage to its roof and outbuildings.  An elementary school received minor roof damage.  The city's hospital suffered heavy damage.  A portion of the hospital's roof collapsed, winds blew out numerous windows, and two air handling units were torn from the roof.  A local state of emergency was declared.  Reports were provided by the Franklin County EMA and Sheriff, and damage surveyed by the NWS.

This developed strong north to northeasterly winds and a coastal trough along the Florida east coast.  These conditions are known locally as a northeaster. High surf and minor beach erosion occurred in Duval and Nassau Counties of northeast Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Minor coastal flooding and Beach erosion with seven (7) foot breakers.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Funnel cloud.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A funnel cloud was observed for a couple of minutes near Niceville.  The funnel never touched the ground.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A squall line moved out of the Florida Big Bend and Panhandle with some severe weather in the Suwannee Valley. Damage was generally confined to trees and power lines down.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power Lines blown down near U.S. Highway 27 and County Road 49.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Cold air and northeast winds of 10 to 15 mph behind an an early season arctic cold front produced wind chills of 20 to 25 degrees Fahrenheit.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Overnight low temperatures reached 32 in Bronson, 31 six miles southeast of Chiefland, and 33 twelve miles southwest of Chiefland. Freezing temperatures only lasted about an hour so this was not considered a freeze event.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: On December 24th an area of surface low pressure developed in the Gulf of Mexico as a result of a strong upper level trough and an associated powerful branch of the subtropical jet. As this low pressure system moved into Florida, a squall line developed over the eastern Gulf of Mexico and then moved rapidly into Florida . The squall line spawned severe thunderstorms and tornadoes moving east and northeast at around 50 MPH.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An F0 tornado touched down briefly in the Brittany Estates mobile home community just north of Highway 441 in Leesburg. The tornado moved rapidly east and damaged roofs and awnings of 24 residences.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: On December 24th an area of surface low pressure developed in the Gulf of Mexico as a result of a strong upper level trough and an associated powerful branch of the subtropical jet. As this low pressure system moved into Florida, a squall line developed over the eastern Gulf of Mexico and then moved rapidly into Florida . The squall line spawned severe thunderstorms and tornadoes moving east and northeast at around 50 MPH.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An F2 tornado touched down just west of North Stone Street in Deland and moved northeast crossing Highway 17. It moved across the Fernwood mobile home community and continued northeast over Meadowlea Estates mobile home community. It lifted just north of Carter Road. Fifty two residences were destroyed and 162 were damaged. Five people were seriously injured.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of severe thunderstorms ahead of a cold front moved through the Florida Big Bend Friday evening, October 27th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A few trees and power lines down in the Montivideo subdivision.  Another tree fell on Palgs Road.  Reported by a county 911 dispatcher.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A squall line produced several reports of wind damage as well as hail during the afternoon of the 15th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds tore the eaves off a home on U.S. Highway 231 and overturned two semi-trailers.  A few trees were toppled.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of thunderstorms ahead of a cold front moved across the eastern Gulf of Mexico and through much of Florida. Doppler radar showed bow echoes with several of the storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The tornado hit the Tampa Bay Golf and Country Club which is located a few miles west of San Antonio or about 9.5 miles west southwest of Dade City.  Radar showed the tornado occurred on the north side of a small bow echo that was moving to the northeast and caused additional damage north of State Road 52. The tornado caused $3.5 million in damage, two injuries, 101 damaged homes, 21 of which were considered heavily damaged, and 6 destroyed. Most of the damage was rated F0 or F1 on the Fuita tornado scale. However, damage rated as F2 occurred on the 2900 block of Caddyshack Lane.  The damage looked like high end F1 damage, but the 1999 home had hurricane straps, a reinforced garage door, and other protective measures making it stronger than the average home.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Afternoon thunderstorms accompanied by very heavy rains caused the roof of an apartment complex in Sunrise to leak.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains caused the roof of the Quail Run apartment complex to leak, with water entering about 48 apartments. A total of 33 people were left homeless as a result of the water.|The roof was under repair due to damage from Hurricane Wilma in October 2005.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak frontal boundary across central Florida produced thunderstorms, hail and a small F0 tornado.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A small F0 tornado touched down briefly in Apopka blowing down small trees, power lines and taking the flat roof surface off a small apartment building.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Wind event along coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: High winds from a thunderstorm damaged several roofs and skirting was blown from manufactured homes near East Bay Heights.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong southerly winds developed ahead of a cold front and caused low level shear to increase across the area.  As the front moved east the shear diminished.  By the time the storms moved into the Florida Panhandle, the event had transitioned from a tornado to a wind event.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wind speed gust of 60 MPH was measured at Pensacola Regional Airport.  No damage was found around the airport from the thunderstorm gust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The squall line which brought tornadoes, torrential rain and damaging winds to southeast Alabama and the Florida Panhandle weakened as it moved east into the Big Bend.  However, a few of the thunderstorms still produced severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hail estimated up to the size of baseballs fell north of Adams Beach.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms along and ahead of a strong cold front produced wind gusts of 40 to 61 MPH around the Tampa Bay area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wind gust of 61 MPH from the south southwest was recorded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Warm moist, unstable air in advance of approaching cold front produced several tornadoes across the eastern Florida Panhandle on Friday afternoon, October 27th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A waterspout came ashore near Phillips Inlet and U.S. Highway 98.  The brief tornado flipped a large truck on its side in the Winn Dixie parking lot before lifting over Powell Lake.  Reported by a SKYWARN storm spotter.
